headline: USAR SURGES 13%: $2.8B Serra Verde Deal Creates First Mine-to-Magnet Rare Earth Producer Outside Asia
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ:USAR) announced a $2.8 billion deal to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde — the only non-Asian producer capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths (Nd, Pr, Dy, Tb) at scale. The deal includes a 15-year U.S. government offtake agreement and targets $1.8B EBITDA by 2030. USAR closed up 13.18% on the announcement.

headline: RIO TINTO Q1 COPPER UP 9%: 229,000 Tonnes as Oyu Tolgoi Ramps; Iron Ore Posts Second-Highest Q1 Since 2018
snippet: Rio Tinto reported Q1 2026 copper production of 229,000 tonnes, up 9% year-on-year, driven by the Oyu Tolgoi underground ramp-up. Pilbara iron ore output climbed 13% despite cyclone disruptions. Full-year production and cost guidance were left unchanged — a positive signal for copper supply forecasts.

headline: CEASEFIRE CRACKING: US Seizes Iranian Ship, Hormuz Fears Return — Gold Slides to $4,782, Copper Retreats
snippet: The U.S. seized an Iranian cargo ship attempting to run its blockade, threatening the fragile two-week ceasefire and reigniting Strait of Hormuz closure fears. Gold fell 0.81% to $4,782/oz, copper retreated from two-month highs, and the dollar index strengthened. A second round of negotiations is still scheduled before the truce expires.

headline: SILVER STREAK: Four Straight Weekly Gains Push Metal to $82/oz — Up More Than $46 Year-Over-Year
snippet: Silver climbed roughly 5% last week to close at $82/oz, marking its fourth consecutive weekly advance. The metal sits nearly 30% above its March low and has gained more than $46/oz over the past year. Industrial demand from solar panels and EVs continues to underpin buying even as geopolitical uncertainty swings prices week to week.

headline: SIX YEARS IN THE RED: Silver Institute Projects 46.3M Oz Deficit in 2026; 762M Oz Drained From Global Reserves Since 2021
snippet: The Silver Institute's updated 2026 survey projects a 46.3 million ounce supply deficit — the sixth consecutive year of structural shortfall. Physical investment demand is forecast to surge 20% to a three-year high of 227 Moz. Total supply rises just 1.5% to a decade-high 1.05 billion ounces — not enough to close the gap.

headline: RATIO REVERSAL: Gold-Silver Ratio Falls From April High of 105 — Analysts Cite Historic Precedent for Sharp Silver Outperformance
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April — one of its most extreme readings in decades — and has begun contracting toward the long-run average near 60:1. Past reversals from such extremes, including 2008–09 and 2016, preceded sustained silver outperformance of 30–50%. Silver miners with operational leverage stand to benefit most if the pattern holds.

headline: CHINA SILVER DEMAND: Beijing Imports 790+ Tonnes in Early 2026 as Solar Buildout Creates Structural Base-Load Buying
snippet: China imported more than 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026, with solar panel manufacturing the dominant driver. The GoldSeek Gold SWOT analysis highlights this as a structural baseload demand that prior supply models underestimated. With Chinese solar installation targets unchanged, this demand floor is unlikely to reverse even if monetary buying cools.

headline: JUNIOR COPPER TURNS CASH-FLOW POSITIVE: Magna Mining (TSXV:NICU) Posts $3.3M Cash Margin at Sudbury McCreedy West in Q4
snippet: Magna Mining (TSXV:NICU) delivered a $3.3 million positive cash margin in Q4 2025 at its McCreedy West copper-nickel-PGM mine in Sudbury — the strongest quarterly production since the company took over the asset. Processed 84,954 tons at 3.41% CuEq. The company closed the year with $55.9 million cash and reiterated 2026 operational guidance.

headline: FIRST DRILL IN 40 YEARS: Aventis Energy Returns 23m of Radioactive Intervals Across 10 Holes at Corvo Uranium Project, Saskatchewan
snippet: Aventis Energy concluded its inaugural drill program at its Corvo Project near Wollaston Lake in northeastern Saskatchewan — the first drilling at Corvo in more than 40 years. Ten holes returned 23 metres of cumulative radioactive intervals across multiple target areas, confirming critical metal fertility. Assay results are pending.

headline: ATHABASCA EARN-IN: Global Uranium Locks 20% of Astro Project Where 25km Conductive Corridor Awaits First Drill Test
snippet: Global Uranium Corp. has satisfied Phase 1 conditions to earn a 20% interest in its Astro Project in the eastern Athabasca Basin, Saskatchewan. A 2025 ZTEM airborne survey identified a 25-kilometre conductive corridor with several priority structural targets. An Ambient Noise Tomography survey is underway to finalize drill targeting ahead of the next campaign.
